blocks.text: DTC P0351: No. 1 cylinder ignition primary circuit failure DTC P0352: No. 2 cylinder ignition primary circuit failure DTC P0353: No. 3 Cylinder ignition primary circuit failure

The ignition coils are controlled by the PGM-FI ECU for optimal timing of ignition. The PGM-FI ECU detects failures during engine operation (after cylinder discrimination is completed). The ignition coil driver has an internal fault diagnosis function and sends fault information to the CPU; the PGM-FI ECU determines the fault based on the fault diagnosis information from the driver. If the fault diagnosis information from the ignition coil driver continues for more than a set period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a fault and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Ignition Coil Failure information from the driver for more than 25 consecutive cycles. (counted once per engine cycle)

items.text: Disconnection of cord between PGM-FI ECU and No.1 ignition coil (IGN COIL 1 line)
Ground fault in code (IGN COIL 1 line) between PGM-FI ECU and No.1 ignition coil
Disconnection of cord between PGM-FI ECU and No.2 ignition coil (IGN COIL 2 line)
Ground fault between PGM-FI ECU and No.2 ignition coil (IGN COIL 2 line)
Disconnection of cord between PGM-FI ECU and No.3 ignition coil (IGN COIL 3 line)
Ground fault between PGM-FI ECU and No.3 ignition coil code (IGN COIL 3 line)
Disconnection of ignition coil cord (power supply line)
Disconnection of cord (ground line) between ignition coil and body

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
